GET__business-rules__models.description: Return registered models with their columns and relationships.

POST__business-rules__validate.description: Validate a rule payload without saving it.

GET__business-rules.description: List all business rules. Returns the full list of configured rules with their IDs and conditions.

GET__business-rules__rule_id.description: Return a single business rule by its ID.

POST__business-rules.description: Create a new business rule. Input: rule definition payload. Returns the created rule with its assigned ID.

PUT__business-rules__rule_id.description: Update an existing business rule by its ID. Input: updated rule definition payload. Returns the updated rule.

DELETE__business-rules__rule_id.description: Delete a business rule by its ID. Returns the deleted rule ID.

GET__exceptionalert__banner-alerts.description:
Return Exception Alerts for the sticky footer banner.

Reads the configured urgency levels from
[exception_alerts] exception_alerts_urgency_levels_for_banner
in jm_config.ini (default: "Critical, Very Critical").

Returns distinct alert descriptions with their counts,
excluding alerts with status "Solved".

Uses ORM notation — no raw FK column access.

GET__exceptionalert__by-model-type__enti.description:
Return Exception Alerts associated to entities of the given model type.

Uses an ORM join through the polymorphic alert_applies_to_object
relation and the Entities table, without referencing raw FK column
names as strings in the query logic (ORM column attributes are used).

Only alerts whose status is not "Solved" are returned.

:param entity_type: The model type name (e.g. "Inventory", "Item").

GET__journeys.description: List all journey definitions. Returns paginated journey definitions with their steps and transitions.

GET__journeys__journey_id.description: Return a single journey definition by its ID.

POST__journeys.description: Create a new journey definition. Input: journey definition with steps and transitions. Returns the created journey with its assigned ID.

PUT__journeys__journey_id.description: Update an existing journey definition by its ID. Returns the updated journey.

DELETE__journeys__journey_id.description: Delete a journey definition by its ID.

GET__journey-instances.description: List all journey instances for the current user. Returns paginated active and completed journey instances.

GET__journey-instances__instance_id.description: Return a single journey instance by its ID, including its current step and state.

POST__journey-instances.description: Start a new journey instance for the current user. Input: journey ID and optional initial state. Returns the created instance.

PUT__journey-instances__instance_id.description: Advance or update a journey instance by its ID. Input: updated instance state and current step. Returns the updated instance.

DELETE__journey-instances__instance_id.description: Delete a journey instance by its ID.

GET__iqvigilant__license__pool.description: Return the IQVigilant license pool — consumed by the frontend LicenseContext.

GET__iqvigilant__license__installation-i.description: Return this installation's UUID (displayed in the License Management page).

GET__iqvigilant__license__keys.description: List all IQVigilant license keys (admin only).

POST__iqvigilant__license__keys.description: Submit a new IQVigilant license JWT (admin only).

PATCH__iqvigilant__license__keys__key_id.description: Deactivate a IQVigilant license key (admin only).

GET__mechanism__mechanism_id__results_li.description:
Return the list of MechanismResult entities for a Mechanism.

Uses ORM relationship traversal — no raw FK columns.

GET__mechanism__mechanism_id__nl_sentenc.description:
Return the NL Sentence entities linked to a Mechanism.

Uses ORM relationship traversal — no raw FK columns.

POST__mechanism__mechanism_id__execute.description:
Execute all NL Sentences linked to a Mechanism (discovery-only).

Parses NL Sentences and discovers entity type names,
persisting them as MechanismResult entities.

Does NOT execute commands against actual data — that requires
execute_mechanism_for_exception_alert called from the
exception_alert factory.

Uses ORM — no raw FK columns.

GET__nlchat__nl_chat_eid__custom_content.description: Return the rendered HTML for an NLChat (non-streaming).

Stream the rendered HTML for an NLChat progressively.

Each NLSentence block is yielded as soon as its answers are ready,
so the frontend can render sentence-by-sentence rather than waiting
for the entire chat.

GET__nlsentence__nl_sentence_eid__custom.description: Return the rendered HTML for an NLSentence.

PATCH__nlchat__nl_chat_eid__sync_nlsente.description: Update NLChat settings and propagate to linked NLSentences.

PATCH__nlsentence__nl_sentence_eid.description:
Partial-update an NLSentence by its own eid (no NLChat context).

Used by the NLSentence show page's inline-editable fields, which post the
changed attributes directly for the sentence being viewed.

PATCH__nlchat__nl_chat_eid__nlsentence__.description: Update a specific NLSentence linked to an NLChat.

DELETE__nlsentence__nl_sentence_eid__ans.description:
Clear the cached NLAnswer answers for a single NLSentence.

Deletes the NLAnswer records associated to the sentence so the next time the
page is refreshed (or the sentence is recomputed) the answer is re-processed,
regardless of which method originally answered it.

DELETE__nlchat__nl_chat_eid__answers.description:
Clear the cached NLAnswer answers for ALL NLSentences of an NLChat.

Deletes every NLAnswer record associated to each of the chat's NLSentences, so
on the next refresh/recompute (or when a new sentence is added and all answers
are recomputed) every answer is re-processed from scratch.

POST__nlchat__nl_chat_eid__nlsentence.description:
Create a new NLSentence and link it to an NLChat.

Inherits the parent NLChat's current display settings (view type, thresholds,
row limit, etc.) so the sentence starts in sync without a manual sync call.

GET__agentic-tools.description:
Available agent tools for building deterministic plan steps.

Returns a combined palette of:
1. User-defined tools from ``config/nl_tools/nl_tools.csv``
2. Auto-discovered API tools from ``api_tools_registry.json`` (enabled only)

The plan builder also lets the user type any other tool name
(e.g. the generic NLP/schema tools instantiated at runtime),
so this list is a palette of suggestions rather than an exhaustive set.

POST__deterministic-plans__auto-detect-m.description:
Auto-detect parameter mappings between two tools for a plan transition.

Accepts ``{ from_tool_name, to_tool_name, from_step_id }`` and returns a
list of ``{ from_param, to_param, from_step }`` mappings where the from
tool's output parameters overlap with the to tool's input parameters.

Delegates to ``plan_savings.compute_param_mappings`` — the same logic used
by the deterministic executor at runtime — by building the specific tool
objects from available registries.

POST__deterministic-plans__tool-params.description:
Return input and output parameter names for a single tool.

Accepts ``{ tool_name }`` and returns ``{ inputs: [...], outputs: [...] }``.
Outputs include declared input params (which may also appear in responses)
plus path-segment-derived field names from the tool key.

GET__deterministic-plans.description: List all saved deterministic execution plans. Returns plan keys, descriptions, and step graphs.

GET__deterministic-plans__plan_key.description: Return a single deterministic plan by its key, including all step nodes and transitions.

POST__deterministic-plans.description:
Create a registered plan from the Deterministic Plan Builder.

Body: { name, description?, status?, start_node?, nodes }. ``nodes`` is the
registry node map (id → { id, tool, purpose, transitions: { default, paths } })
that the deterministic planner/executor reads.

PUT__deterministic-plans__plan_key.description: Update an existing deterministic plan by its key. Input: updated step graph and metadata. Returns the saved plan.

DELETE__deterministic-plans__plan_key.description: Delete a deterministic plan by its key.

POST__deterministic-plans__bulk.description: Perform a bulk action (enable, disable, delete) on multiple deterministic plans.

GET__potential-plans.description: List all LLM-generated candidate plans pending promotion. Returns plan keys and step graphs.

GET__potential-plans__plan_key.description: Return a single candidate plan by its key.

DELETE__potential-plans__plan_key.description: Delete a candidate plan by its key.

POST__potential-plans__bulk.description: Perform a bulk action on multiple candidate plans.

POST__potential-plans__plan_key__promote.description: Promote a candidate plan to the active deterministic plan registry.

GET__deterministic-plans-savings-chart.description:
Monthly token-savings Plotly chart (HTML) for the Deterministic Plan Manager.

Distinct path (not under ``/deterministic-plans/{plan_key}``) so it is not
captured by the dynamic plan-key route. Returns rendered Plotly HTML with four
savings traces + two dotted fully-agentic reference lines. The request locale is
set from ``lang``/Accept-Language so every _() call in the chart (title, axis,
legend, empty-state) renders in the page language.

Optional ``start_date``/``end_date`` (ISO date strings) filter the execution
data to a date range.

GET__deterministic-plans-savings-by-plan.description:
Per-plan token-savings Plotly chart (HTML) for the Deterministic Plan Manager.

Same metrics/traces as the monthly chart, but the savings are summarised by
``executed_plan_name`` instead of by month. Distinct path so it is not captured
by the dynamic ``/deterministic-plans/{plan_key}`` route. The request locale is
set from ``lang``/Accept-Language so the chart texts render in the page language.

Optional ``start_date``/``end_date`` (ISO date strings) filter the execution
data to a date range.

GET__executions-savings-chart.description:
Monthly usage/savings Plotly chart (HTML) for the Executions Usage & Savings page.

Aggregates all four token-stamped execution modes (native SVO/SRO,
deterministic plan, hybrid, workflow agent) side by side, in two stacked
subplots (tokens, then estimated monetary value).

Optional ``start_date``/``end_date`` (ISO date strings) filter the execution
data to a date range.

GET__executions-savings-by-plan-chart.description:
Per-plan (or per-bucket, for plan-less methods) usage/savings Plotly chart (HTML).

Same metrics/traces as the monthly chart, summarised by ``executed_plan_name``
where present, falling back to the bucket's display label for methods that
don't register a plan (native SVO/SRO, workflow agent).

Optional ``start_date``/``end_date`` (ISO date strings) filter the execution
data to a date range.

GET__executions-savings-by-method-chart.description:
Per-answering-method breakdown usage/savings Plotly chart (HTML).

One category row per bucket (native SVO/SRO, deterministic plan, hybrid,
workflow agent), aggregated across the whole filtered date range -- a
direct side-by-side comparison of how much each answering method uses vs
saves, independent of when it ran.

Optional ``start_date``/``end_date`` (ISO date strings) filter the execution
data to a date range.

GET__executions-savings-summary.description: KPI summary for the Executions Usage & Savings dashboard cards.

GET__deterministic-plans-savings-summary.description:
KPI summary for the Deterministic Savings dashboard cards.

Returns total tokens saved, active plan count, and total executions.
Optional ``start_date``/``end_date`` filter the execution data.

GET__nl-analytical-queries-savings-by-mo.description:
Monthly estimated-token-savings Plotly chart (HTML) for the NL Analytical
Queries Reporting page.

Returns rendered Plotly HTML with two bar traces (input/output tokens saved)
summarised by month. The request locale is set from ``lang``/Accept-Language
so every _() call in the chart (title, axis, legend, empty-state) renders in
the page language.

GET__nl-analytical-queries-savings-by-ac.description:
Per-action-type estimated-token-savings Plotly chart (HTML) for the NL
Analytical Queries Reporting page.

Same metrics as the by-month chart, but summarised by analytical action type
(query / chart / card / summarize / decompose) instead of by month. The
request locale is set from ``lang``/Accept-Language so the chart texts render
in the page language.

GET__nl-answers-confidence-by-month-char.description:
Monthly average answer-confidence Plotly chart (HTML) for the NL Answers
Confidence Probability reporting page.

Returns rendered Plotly HTML with a single bar trace (average confidence)
summarised by month (MMM-YYYY). The request locale is set from
``lang``/Accept-Language so the chart texts render in the page language.

GET__nl-answers-confidence-by-method-typ.description:
Average confidence by answering method type Plotly chart (HTML).

Groups answers into deterministic / probabilistic / hybrid method-type
families and shows their average confidence. The request locale is set from
``lang``/Accept-Language so the chart texts render in the page language.

GET__nl-answers-confidence-by-plan-chart.description:
Average confidence by deterministic plan Plotly chart (HTML).

Only NLAnswers associated with a deterministic plan are included. The
request locale is set from ``lang``/Accept-Language so the chart texts
render in the page language.

GET__nl-answers-confidence-by-database-c.description:
Average confidence by database alias Plotly chart (HTML).

Groups answers by the database they were answered from. Null database
values are labeled "Local App Database". The request locale is set from
``lang``/Accept-Language so the chart texts render in the page language.

GET__nl-answers-confidence-by-database-m.description:
Average confidence by database × method type crosstab Plotly chart (HTML).

Renders as a grouped horizontal bar chart where each database has bars
broken down by method-type family (deterministic / probabilistic / hybrid).
The request locale is set from ``lang``/Accept-Language so the chart texts
render in the page language.

Average confidence by database × specific answering method crosstab Plotly
chart (HTML).

Renders as a grouped horizontal bar chart where each database has bars
broken down by specific answering method (native SVO/SRO, deterministic
plan, agentic + deterministic, workflow agent, direct LLM, pandasAI).
The request locale is set from ``lang``/Accept-Language so the chart texts
render in the page language.

GET__nl-analytical-queries-savings-summa.description:
KPI summary for the NL Analytical Queries Reporting dashboard cards.

Returns total estimated tokens saved and total queries answered natively.
Optional ``start_date``/``end_date`` filter the execution data.

GET__nl-answers-confidence-summary.description:
KPI summary for the Answers Confidence Reporting dashboard cards.

Returns overall average confidence, deterministic vs probabilistic average
confidence, total answers, and the deterministic share. Optional
``start_date``/``end_date`` filter the execution data.

GET__deterministic-plans-confidence-summ.description:
KPI summary for the Deterministic Plan Manager confidence cards.

Returns average confidence, deterministic plans confidence, probabilistic
(agentic) confidence, and total answers — filtered to only deterministic plans,
agentic AI, and hybrid answering methods. Optional ``start_date``/``end_date``
filter the execution data.

POST__deterministic-plans__plan_key__est.description:
Set an APPROXIMATE fully-agentic baseline (F) on a plan, from its structure.

Used by the Builder's "Approximate estimate" option for hand-authored plans
that were never produced by a real agentic run. The estimate derives from
measured baselines (avg tokens/step) with a configurable fallback.

POST__deterministic-plans__plan_key__mea.description:
Measure a plan's fully-agentic baseline (F) with one real full-agentic run.

Body: ``{ sample_question }``. Runs the representative question once through the
fully-agentic agent, capturing its input/output tokens, and stores them on the
plan as a measured baseline. Accurate but costs one LLM run.

GET__api-tools.description:
List all discovered API tools (paginated, searchable).

Returns the full registry including disabled and orphaned tools,
so admins can manage them from the UI.

GET__api-tools__tool_key:path.description: Get a single API tool's full metadata.

PATCH__api-tools__tool_key:path.description: Update an API tool's enabled flag and/or allowed_roles.

PUT__api-tools__tool_key:path.description:
Full update of an existing API tool's editable metadata fields.

All writable fields can be changed: ``http_method``, ``path``, ``summary``,
``description``, ``module``, ``enabled``, ``allowed_roles``, ``parameters``,
``request_body``, ``response_type``, ``base_url``.
Read-only fields (``source``, ``first_discovered``, ``status``) are preserved.

DELETE__api-tools__tool_key:path.description:
Delete a manually-added API tool from the registry.

Auto-discovered tools cannot be deleted — disable them instead.
Checks for deterministic plan dependencies before deleting.

GET__api-tools__check-usage__tool_key:pa.description: Check whether any deterministic plans reference a given tool key.

POST__api-tools__refresh.description: Force a re-scan of all modules and regenerate the registry.

GET__api-tools-candidates.description:
List all pre-configured API tool candidates from the shipped catalog.

Supports optional filtering by search (matches tool_key, summary, and
description), category, and source_type (external_api or builtin).

GET__api-tools-candidates__tool_key:path.description: Get a single candidate API tool's full metadata by its tool_key.

POST__api-tools-candidates__promote.description:
Promote one or more candidate tools into the active API tools registry.

Accepts a JSON body with ``tool_keys`` (list of candidate tool_key
values to promote).  Returns counts of promoted, skipped
(already present), and not-found keys.

GET__api-tools-candidates__categories.description: Return the distinct category values across all candidates.

POST__api-tools__test.description:
Test a tool by sending a real HTTP request to its configured endpoint.

* **GET** — always executes (read-only, safe).
* **POST / PUT / PATCH / DELETE** — only executes when
  ``confirm_mutation`` is ``true``; otherwise returns a warning.

POST__api-tools__discover.description:
Auto-discover an API endpoint's parameters and metadata.

Accepts ``url`` (required) and ``http_method`` (optional, default ``GET``).
Tries these strategies in order:

1. **Heuristic URL analysis** — inspects path segments for numeric,
   UUID-like, and short-alphanumeric values, converting them to
   path/query parameters automatically (no OpenAPI spec needed).
2. Look for an OpenAPI/Swagger spec at the base URL.
3. Send an ``OPTIONS`` request to inspect CORS headers.
4. Send the actual HTTP request and infer response structure.

GET__dashboard-chart-panels.description:
Return the available IQVigilant dashboard chart panels.

Each panel describes a Plotly chart that can be added as a cell to the
host app's /dashboard page. The ``model`` field uses the naming convention
``iqvigilant:chart:<name>`` so it never collides with real DB model names.

POST__dashboard-seed-charts.description:
Add IQVigilant reporting chart cells to the host app's dashboard.

Accepts an optional ``tab_name`` (default ``"IQVigilant Reporting"``) and
optional ``panel_models`` list to select specific panels. If omitted, all
available panels are added. Cells are written to views_preferences.json.

GET__reporting-page-config.description: Auto-seed and return the SectionsGrid config + chart metadata for a reporting page.

GET__ai-config__ini.description: Return the AI-relevant config keys with their current values and metadata.

PUT__ai-config__ini.description:
Update AI-relevant config keys in the host's override jm_config.ini.

Body: { "updates": { "nlp": { "answer_asks_via_jm": "true", ... }, ... } }
Only writes keys that differ from the shipped package defaults.
Uses tempfile + atomic rename for safety.

GET__ai-config__synonyms__locale.description: Return the synonyms CSV rows for a given locale.

PUT__ai-config__synonyms__locale.description:
Overwrite the host's synonyms CSV for a given locale.

Body: { "rows": [{ "term": "...", "synonym": "...", "keyword_type": "..." }, ...] }

GET__ai-config__dbconnectors.description: List all configured external DbConnectors with summary fields.

GET__ai-config__plans-summary.description: Return summary KPIs for deterministic plans.

GET__ai-config__api-tools-summary.description: Return summary KPIs for the API Tools Manager.

GET__ai-vigilance__summary.description: Return a combined KPI summary from all four vigilance sources.

GET__personalization-summary__business-r.description: Return KPI summary for the Business Rules section.

GET__personalization-summary__journeys.description: Return KPI summary for the User Journeys section.

GET__personalization-summary__pageconfig.description: Return KPI summary for the Page Configuration section.

GET__personalization-summary__templates.description: Return KPI summary for the Template Builder section.

POST__dbconnector__connector_id__test-co.description:
Test connectivity to a DbConnector's target database.

Builds a SQLAlchemy connection URL from the DbConnector's fields
(server type, host, port, database name, username, password) and
attempts to open a connection.  Returns success metadata or a
descriptive error.

Uses ORM object notation — no raw FK columns.

GET__system-config.description:
Return system configuration keys with current values and metadata.

Reads values from both jm_config.ini (via jm_obtain_config) and
veloiq.toml [views] (via read_views_config). Returns a flat list
of config items for the System Configuration console frontend.

For toml-sourced keys not yet present in veloiq.toml, returns the
framework default value so the frontend always shows a sensible
starting point.

Requires CONFIGURE_LAYOUT permission in the user's role.

PUT__system-config.description:
Update system configuration keys across jm_config.ini and veloiq.toml.

Body: { "updates": { "log_up_to_relevance": "2", "plain_color_base_hex": "#1e708a", ... } }

Keys with source "ini" are written to config/jm_config.ini using the atomic
tempfile pattern. Keys with source "toml" are written to veloiq.toml [views].

Requires CONFIGURE_LAYOUT permission in the user's role.

GET__pageconfig__configs.description: Return all page configurations keyed by '{model}:{view_type}'.

GET__pageconfig__configs__model__view_ty.description: Return the page configuration for one model + view type (or empty).

PUT__pageconfig__configs__model__view_ty.description:
Persist the page configuration for one model + view type.

Stores the 'Details' tab sections plus any additional named tabs (each tab
holds its own sections). Sections hold entries (fields, relations, or
embedded nl_sentences) positioned in a row/column grid.

DELETE__pageconfig__configs__model__view.description: Remove the page configuration for one model + view type.

GET__pageconfig__templates.description: Return metadata for all available templates (built-in + custom).

GET__pageconfig__templates__template_nam.description: Return a single template definition by name.

GET__pageconfig__templates__resolve__mod.description:
Resolve the template assigned to a model + view_type into ViewConfigRow[].

Priority: explicit page config > template assignment > nothing.

POST__pageconfig__templates__assign.description:
Assign a template to one or more pages.

Body: {"template": "...", "parameters": {}, "targets": [{"model":"T","view_type":"show"}]}

POST__pageconfig__templates__assign__pre.description: Preview bulk assignment impact without persisting.

PUT__pageconfig__templates__template_nam.description:
Create or update a template definition.

Saves the template JSON to page_templates/{name}.json in the host app's
working directory. Custom templates override built-in templates with the
same name.

Required fields: name, applicable_view_types, tabs

GET__pageconfig__templates__template_nam.description: Return all pages assigned to a given template.

DELETE__pageconfig__templates__template_.description:
Delete a custom template and unassign it from all pages.

Built-in templates (shipped with the package) cannot be deleted.
Removing a template also removes it from page_configs.json for all
affected pages via re-materialization.

GET__pageconfig__templates__assignments_.description: Return all current template assignments as {key: template_name}.
